en: Then he brought me back to the outer gate of the sanctuary, which faces east. And it was shut.
zh: 他又領我回到聖所朝東的外門，這門關閉著。

en: And the LORD said to me, "This gate shall remain shut; it shall not be opened, and no one shall enter by it, for the LORD, the God of Israel, has entered by it. Therefore it shall remain shut.
zh: 耶和華對我說：“這門必須關閉，不可打開；任何人也不得從那裡進入；因為耶和華以色列的 神已經從那裡進入，所以這門必須關閉。

en: Only the prince may sit in it to eat bread before the LORD. He shall enter by way of the vestibule of the gate, and shall go out by the same way."
zh: 只有君王可以坐在裡面，在耶和華面前吃餅；他必須從門廊進入，也必須從原路出來。”

en: Then he brought me by way of the north gate to the front of the temple, and I looked, and behold, the glory of the LORD filled the temple of the LORD. And I fell on my face.
zh: 他又領我經過北門來到殿前；我觀看，見耶和華的榮耀充滿耶和華的殿，我就臉伏在地。

en: And the LORD said to me, "Son of man, mark well, see with your eyes, and hear with your ears all that I shall tell you concerning all the statutes of the temple of the LORD and all its laws. And mark well the entrance to the temple and all the exits from the sanctuary.
zh: 耶和華對我說：“人子啊！我所告訴你耶和華殿的一切規條和法則，你都要留心，用眼看，用耳聽，並要留心聖殿的入口和聖所的所有出口。

en: And say to the rebellious house, to the house of Israel, Thus says the Lord GOD: O house of Israel, enough of all your abominations,
zh: 你要對悖逆的以色列家說：‘主耶和華這樣說：以色列家啊！你們所行一切可憎的事，該夠了吧！

en: in admitting foreigners, uncircumcised in heart and flesh, to be in my sanctuary, profaning my temple, when you offer to me my food, the fat and the blood. You have broken my covenant, in addition to all your abominations.
zh: 你們除了行這一切可憎的事之外，還在你們把脂肪和血獻給我作食物的時候，把身心都未受割禮的外族人帶進我的聖所，玷污了我的殿，違背了我的約。

en: And you have not kept charge of my holy things, but you have set others to keep my charge for you in my sanctuary.
zh: 你們自己沒有看守我的聖物，卻委派別人在我的聖所裡代你們看守。’”

en: "Thus says the Lord GOD: No foreigner, uncircumcised in heart and flesh, of all the foreigners who are among the people of Israel, shall enter my sanctuary.
zh: 主耶和華這樣說：“以色列人中一切身心都未受割禮的外族人，都不可進入我的聖所。

en: But the Levites who went far from me, going astray from me after their idols when Israel went astray, shall bear their punishment.
zh: 以色列人走迷了路的時候，有利未人遠離我，走迷了路。他們離開我去隨從他們的偶像，他們必擔當自己的罪孽。

en: They shall be ministers in my sanctuary, having oversight at the gates of the temple and ministering in the temple. They shall slaughter the burnt offering and the sacrifice for the people, and they shall stand before the people, to minister to them.
zh: 他們要在我的聖所作僕役，看管殿門，在聖殿中供職；他們要為人民宰殺燔祭牲和其他祭牲，又要站在人民面前侍候他們。

en: Because they ministered to them before their idols and became a stumbling block of iniquity to the house of Israel, therefore I have sworn concerning them, declares the Lord GOD, and they shall bear their punishment.
zh: 因為這些利未人曾在他們的偶像前侍候這人民，成了陷以色列家在罪孽中的絆腳石。因此，我向他們舉手起誓，他們必要擔當自己的罪孽。這是主耶和華的宣告。

en: They shall not come near to me, to serve me as priest, nor come near any of my holy things and the things that are most holy, but they shall bear their shame and the abominations that they have committed.
zh: 他們不可親近我，作我的祭司，也不可親近我的一切聖物，或至聖之物。他們必要擔當自己的羞辱和他們所行可憎之事的報應。

en: Yet I will appoint them to keep charge of the temple, to do all its service and all that is to be done in it.
zh: 然而我要委派他們看守聖殿，辦理聖殿的一切事務，作一切當作的工。

en: "But the Levitical priests, the sons of Zadok, who kept the charge of my sanctuary when the people of Israel went astray from me, shall come near to me to minister to me. And they shall stand before me to offer me the fat and the blood, declares the Lord GOD.
zh: “以色列人走迷了路，離開我的時候，有祭司利未人，就是撒督的子孫，仍然看守我的聖所；只有他們可以親近我，事奉我，侍立在我面前，把脂肪和血獻給我。這是主耶和華的宣告。

en: They shall enter my sanctuary, and they shall approach my table, to minister to me, and they shall keep my charge.
zh: 他們可以進入我的聖所，可以走近我的桌前來事奉我，守我所吩咐的職責。

en: When they enter the gates of the inner court, they shall wear linen garments. They shall have nothing of wool on them, while they minister at the gates of the inner court, and within.
zh: 每逢他們進入內院的門，必須穿上細麻衣；每逢他們在內院的門和聖殿裡供職，他們不可穿上羊毛的衣服。

en: They shall have linen turbans on their heads, and linen undergarments around their waists. They shall not bind themselves with anything that causes sweat.
zh: 他們頭上要戴細麻布的頭巾，腰間要穿細麻布的褲子；不可穿上使身體容易出汗的衣服。

en: And when they go out into the outer court to the people, they shall put off the garments in which they have been ministering and lay them in the holy chambers. And they shall put on other garments, lest they communicate holiness to the people with their garments.
zh: 每逢他們出到外院民眾那裡，就要把供職的衣服脫下，存放在聖殿的房子裡，然後穿上別的衣服，免得因自己的聖衣使人民成聖。

en: They shall not shave their heads or let their locks grow long; they shall surely trim the hair of their heads.
zh: 他們不可剃頭，也不可留長頭髮，只可剪短頭髮。

en: No priest shall drink wine when he enters the inner court.
zh: 所有祭司進入內院的時候，都不可喝酒。

en: They shall not marry a widow or a divorced woman, but only virgins of the offspring of the house of Israel, or a widow who is the widow of a priest.
zh: 他們不可娶寡婦或被休的婦人；只可娶以色列後裔中的處女，或是祭司留下的寡婦。

en: They shall teach my people the difference between the holy and the common, and show them how to distinguish between the unclean and the clean.
zh: 他們要教導我的子民分別聖俗，使他們分辨潔淨的和不潔淨的。

en: In a dispute, they shall act as judges, and they shall judge it according to my judgments. They shall keep my laws and my statutes in all my appointed feasts, and they shall keep my Sabbaths holy.
zh: 他們要在爭訟的事上作判斷，要按著我的典章判斷。他們要在我所有的節期裡謹守我的律法和條例，守我的安息日為聖日。

en: They shall not defile themselves by going near to a dead person. However, for father or mother, for son or daughter, for brother or unmarried sister they may defile themselves.
zh: 他們不可接近死人，玷污自己，但如果死人是他們的父親、母親、兒子、女兒、兄弟或是未嫁的姊妹，他們就可以玷污自己。

en: After he has become clean, they shall count seven days for him.
zh: 祭司潔淨以後，還要再等七天。

en: And on the day that he goes into the Holy Place, into the inner court, to minister in the Holy Place, he shall offer his sin offering, declares the Lord GOD.
zh: 他進入內院，到聖所去，在聖所供職的時候，他要獻上贖罪祭。這是主耶和華的宣告。

en: "This shall be their inheritance: I am their inheritance: and you shall give them no possession in Israel; I am their possession.
zh: “祭司必有產業；我就是他們的產業。你們不可在以色列中給他們基業；我就是他們的基業。

en: They shall eat the grain offering, the sin offering, and the guilt offering, and every devoted thing in Israel shall be theirs.
zh: 素祭、贖罪祭和贖愆祭，他們都可以吃。以色列中所有獻給耶和華之物，都要歸給他們。

en: And the first of all the firstfruits of all kinds, and every offering of all kinds from all your offerings, shall belong to the priests. You shall also give to the priests the first of your dough, that a blessing may rest on your house.
zh: 所有初熟之物中最好的和你們獻上的一切供物，都要歸給祭司；你們也要把最先磨好的麵給祭司，好讓福氣臨到你的家。

en: The priests shall not eat of anything, whether bird or beast, that has died of itself or is torn by wild animals.
zh: 無論是飛鳥或是走獸，凡是自然死的，或是被撕裂的，祭司都不可吃。
